items.title: Webb Telescope Spots Mysterious Explosion That Defies Known Physics
items.summary: <cite index="12-27,12-28">GRB 250702B, detected by NASA's James Webb Space Telescope and a global network of observatories, is a bizarre cosmic explosion that refuses to play by the rules and is leaving scientists scrambling for answers.</cite>

items.title: Scientists Finally Found Hidden Critical Point in Supercooled Water
items.summary: <cite index="12-2,12-3,12-5,12-6">Scientists have finally found a hidden 'critical point' in supercooled water that explains why it behaves so strangely, where two different liquid forms of water merge, triggering powerful fluctuations that affect water even at normal temperatures.</cite>

items.title: Philippine Teams Master 3D Sensor Fusion for Autonomous Robotics
items.summary: <cite index="23-6">By synchronizing 3D LiDAR point clouds with 2D RGB video feeds, specialized Philippine teams provide the centimeter-level ground truth and temporal consistency required for autonomous robots to navigate complex, unstructured human environments with 99.9% reliability.</cite>

items.title: Solar Cells Achieve Impossible 130% Efficiency Through Singlet Fission
items.summary: <cite index="11-25,11-26,11-27">A new solar breakthrough may overcome a long-standing efficiency barrier using a 'spin-flip' metal complex to capture and multiply energy from sunlight through singlet fission, reaching about 130% efficiency with more energy carriers produced than photons absorbed.</cite>

items.title: Quasars Shut Down Star Formation in Distant Galaxies
items.summary: <cite index="12-30,12-31">Scientists found that quasars emit radiation strong enough to shut down star formation in nearby galaxies millions of light-years away, a blazing supermassive black hole influencing far more than its own galaxy.</cite>
